articles.headline: Government Unveils Ambitious 'Vision 2047' Economic Roadmap for Developed India
articles.explainer:
• The Prime Minister's Office officially launched the 'Vision 2047' document, outlining a comprehensive strategy to transform India into a developed nation by its centenary of independence.
• The roadmap emphasizes sustainable economic growth, equitable development, social inclusion, and robust technological advancement across all sectors.
• Key pillars include boosting manufacturing, green energy transition, digital infrastructure expansion, and significant investments in human capital and research.
• Targets include increasing per capita income, reducing poverty to negligible levels, and establishing India as a global leader in innovation and sustainable practices.
• Special focus is given to regional development, empowering states, and leveraging demographic dividend through skill development and job creation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Vision 2047 aims to make India a developed nation by 2047.
Key focus areas include manufacturing, green energy, digital infrastructure, and human capital.
The roadmap targets poverty reduction and global leadership in innovation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted approach of 'Vision 2047' in addressing economic, social, and environmental challenges for India's development.
Critically analyze the potential challenges and implementation strategies required to achieve the goals set out in 'Vision 2047'.
Examine how 'Vision 2047' integrates with existing government flagship schemes and international commitments like SDGs.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of India's growth trajectory towards 2047 with symbols of industry, technology, and sustainability.
articles.trivia: India's 'Vision 2020' document, released in 2003, also outlined a roadmap for development, setting targets for the nation by the year 2020.

articles.headline: Nation Celebrates Teachers' Day, President Honours Educators with National Awards
articles.explainer:
• India commemorated Teachers' Day on September 5th, marking the birth anniversary of Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, India's second President and a revered philosopher-statesman.
• The President of India conferred National Awards to Teachers to distinguished educators from across the country for their outstanding contributions to education.
• The day highlighted the critical role of teachers in shaping the intellectual and moral fabric of society and nation-building.
• Educational institutions nationwide organized various events to honor their teachers, emphasizing the importance of quality education and pedagogical innovation.
• Discussions also revolved around the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and its impact on teacher training and professional development.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Teachers' Day is celebrated on September 5th.
It marks the birth anniversary of Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan.
National Awards to Teachers are conferred by the President of India.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the significance of Teachers' Day in promoting the values of education and acknowledging the role of educators in societal progress.
Discuss the challenges faced by the teaching profession in India and suggest measures for their professional development and welfare.
Analyze how the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 seeks to transform the role and training of teachers in India.
articles.imageDescription: President of India presenting National Awards to Teachers on stage.
articles.trivia: Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan famously requested that his birthday be celebrated as Teachers' Day, instead of individually, to honor the entire teaching community.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearances for Key Infrastructure Projects, Emphasizes Sustainable Development
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment, affirming the validity of environmental clearances granted to several critical national infrastructure projects.
• The ruling underscored the judiciary's approach to balancing economic development with environmental protection, emphasizing the principle of sustainable development.
• The Court directed project proponents to strictly adhere to mitigation measures and compensatory afforestation plans outlined in the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) reports.
• Environmental activists expressed mixed reactions, calling for stronger oversight mechanisms to ensure compliance with environmental norms.
• The judgment is expected to provide clarity and impetus for ongoing and future infrastructure development, particularly in sensitive ecological zones.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court rules on environmental clearances for infrastructure.
Decision emphasizes balancing development with environmental protection.
EIA reports and mitigation measures are crucial for such projects.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the Indian judiciary in upholding environmental governance and promoting sustainable development.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process in India and suggest reforms.
Examine the challenges in balancing large-scale infrastructure development with ecological conservation in a developing economy like India.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel resting on legal documents with a backdrop of green leaves and construction silhouettes, symbolizing environmental law and development.
articles.trivia: India was one of the first countries to make environmental protection a constitutional mandate, with Article 48A and 51A(g) added by the 42nd Amendment.

articles.headline: India Pledges Stronger Cooperation with Global South at Key UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to strengthening cooperation with countries of the Global South at a high-level United Nations forum on development cooperation.
• The External Affairs Minister highlighted India's role as a voice for developing nations, advocating for equitable access to resources, climate finance, and technology transfer.
• Emphasis was placed on South-South cooperation initiatives, particularly in areas like digital public infrastructure, health, and renewable energy.
• India also called for reforms in multilateral institutions to ensure greater representation and responsiveness to the needs of the Global South.
• The forum discussed strategies for accelerating progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) amidst ongoing global challenges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India advocates for Global South at UN forum.
Focus on climate finance, technology transfer, and digital public infrastructure.
Calls for reforms in multilateral institutions.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role as a leader of the Global South and its implications for international relations.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for South-South cooperation in achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
Evaluate the necessity for reforms in global governance structures to better represent the interests of developing countries.
articles.imageDescription: Indian diplomat speaking at a UN General Assembly session, with flags of member states in the background.
articles.trivia: The term 'Global South' broadly refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America, often characterized by developing economies and historical experiences of colonialism.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the fifth consecutive time.
• The decision comes against a backdrop of easing retail inflation, though concerns about food price volatility and global economic uncertainties persist.
• RBI Governor emphasized the need for continued vigilance to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting economic growth.
• The central bank maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, signaling that it is prepared to act if inflation pressures resurface.
• Analysts expect this stability to bolster market confidence and support investment, while carefully monitoring upcoming inflation data.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
The stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Decision influenced by easing inflation but vigilance on food prices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and instruments of monetary policy in India, particularly in the context of inflation targeting.
Analyze the implications of the RBI's 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance on economic growth and financial stability.
Critically evaluate the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in balancing inflation control with growth objectives.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo and a graph showing stable interest rates, representing monetary policy decisions.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Propulsion System for Future Deep Space Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful ground testing of an advanced propulsion system designed for future deep space and interplanetary missions.
• This new technology promises enhanced efficiency and longer mission durations, crucial for ambitious projects like Venus orbiter or Mars sample return missions.
• The development marks a significant step towards India's self-reliance in cutting-edge space technology and reducing dependence on foreign components.
• Scientists highlighted the system's potential to enable heavier payloads and more complex orbital maneuvers, expanding ISRO's capabilities.
• This achievement aligns with India's long-term vision for space exploration and its aspiration to become a leading space power.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested an advanced propulsion system.
Aimed at deep space and interplanetary missions.
Enhances efficiency and mission duration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous propulsion technology for India's future space exploration ambitions.
Analyze how advancements in space technology contribute to national development and strategic autonomy.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for ISRO in undertaking complex deep space missions.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of a spacecraft powered by an advanced propulsion system in deep space.
articles.trivia: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was India's first interplanetary mission and made India the fourth space agency to reach Mars.

articles.headline: Government Rolls Out New National Policy on E-waste Management for Circular Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a comprehensive National Policy on E-waste Management, aiming to streamline collection, dismantling, and recycling processes.
• The policy introduces stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets and promotes the formalization of the informal e-waste sector.
• It emphasizes the principles of a circular economy, encouraging repair, reuse, and refurbishment of electronic goods to minimize waste generation.
• Awareness campaigns will be launched to educate consumers about responsible e-waste disposal and the hazards associated with improper handling.
• The policy also seeks to boost indigenous e-waste recycling infrastructure and foster innovation in material recovery technologies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: New National Policy on E-waste Management launched.
Focus on circular economy principles and EPR.
Aims to formalize informal e-waste sector and boost recycling infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by e-waste in India and analyze how the new national policy addresses these issues.
Explain the concept of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and its effectiveness in managing different waste streams.
Examine the potential of a circular economy model in mitigating environmental degradation and promoting sustainable resource management.
articles.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic devices with recycling symbols, representing e-waste management.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top five e-waste generating countries in the world, with a significant portion handled by the informal sector.

articles.headline: Government Unveils National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0 to Bolster Digital Defenses
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0, updating the previous framework to address evolving digital threats.
• The new strategy focuses on strengthening critical information infrastructure protection, enhancing cyber deterrence capabilities, and fostering international cooperation.
• It emphasizes public-private partnerships, skill development in cybersecurity, and robust incident response mechanisms to combat sophisticated cyberattacks.
• Data privacy and protection of individual information are key components, aligning with ongoing efforts to legislate data protection laws.
• The strategy aims to create a resilient and secure cyberspace for citizens, businesses, and government operations.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0 launched by MeitY.
Focuses on critical infrastructure, cyber deterrence, and international cooperation.
Emphasizes data privacy and public-private partnerships.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and the necessity for a comprehensive national cybersecurity strategy in India.
Analyze the key pillars of the National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0 and its potential impact on India's digital security posture.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity framework, including skill gaps and cross-border cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon overlaying a world map, symbolizing global cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: India's Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) is the national agency for handling cyber security incidents.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Digital Healthcare Adoption
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced that the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has achieved several key milestones, significantly expanding its reach across the country.
• Over 50 crore Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) have been generated, facilitating digital access to health records for millions of citizens.
• The mission has successfully integrated a large number of public and private healthcare facilities into its digital ecosystem, enabling seamless information exchange.
• Teleconsultation services under ABDM have witnessed a surge in utilization, particularly in remote and underserved areas, improving access to medical advice.
• The progress underscores India's commitment to leveraging technology for universal health coverage and creating a robust, interoperable digital health infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential.
prelimsPoints: ABDM has generated over 50 crore ABHA IDs.
Integrates public and private healthcare facilities.
Teleconsultation services have seen increased use.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in revolutionizing healthcare delivery in India.
Analyze the challenges in achieving universal digital health adoption and ensuring data privacy under the ABDM framework.
Evaluate how digital health initiatives like ABDM contribute to strengthening India's public health infrastructure and addressing health inequities.
articles.imageDescription: A smartphone displaying a digital health ID and a stethoscope, symbolizing digital healthcare.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), previously known as the National Digital Health Mission, was launched nationwide in 2021.

articles.headline: Government Boosts MSP for Key Rabi Crops Ahead of Sowing Season, Aims to Support Farmer Incomes
articles.explainer:
• The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) approved an increase in the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for all mandated Rabi crops for the upcoming marketing season.
• The hike in MSP is aimed at ensuring remunerative prices for farmers, encouraging crop diversification, and boosting agricultural productivity.
• The announcement comes ahead of the Rabi sowing season, providing farmers with clarity on price realization for their produce.
• Economists debated the inflationary impact of the MSP hike versus its role in supporting rural incomes and food security.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to doubling farmer incomes and strengthening the agricultural value chain through various policy interventions.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: CCEA approved increased MSP for Rabi crops.
Aims to ensure remunerative prices and encourage diversification.
MSP is a key policy for farmer income and food security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and implications of the Minimum Support Price (MSP) policy for agricultural sustainability and farmer welfare in India.
Analyze the economic and social impact of MSP hikes, considering both farmer incomes and inflationary pressures.
Critically evaluate alternative mechanisms or reforms that could enhance farmer remuneration and reduce market distortions in the agricultural sector.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field with a tractor, symbolizing agricultural productivity and government support.
articles.trivia: The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) recommends MSPs for 22 mandated crops and fair and remunerative price (FRP) for sugarcane.

articles.headline: Panchayati Raj Institutions to Receive Enhanced Funding for Grassroots Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Panchayati Raj announced a significant increase in financial devolution to Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) across states, aimed at bolstering rural development.
• The enhanced funding, based on recommendations from the latest Finance Commission, will empower PRIs to undertake more local infrastructure projects and social welfare programs.
• The move is expected to strengthen grassroots democracy and decentralization, enabling local bodies to respond more effectively to community needs.
• Capacity building programs for elected representatives and officials of PRIs will also be intensified to ensure efficient utilization of funds.
• The initiative aligns with the government's vision of 'Gram Swaraj' and fostering self-reliant villages through participatory governance.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
prelimsPoints: PRIs to receive enhanced funding based on Finance Commission recommendations.
Aims to strengthen grassroots democracy and rural development.
Capacity building for PRI representatives to be intensified.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of financial devolution to Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) in strengthening grassroots democracy and rural development in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by PRIs in effective governance and fund utilization, and suggest measures for their empowerment.
Evaluate the concept of 'Gram Swaraj' in the context of modern India and the role of PRIs in achieving it.
articles.imageDescription: A group of villagers participating in a Gram Sabha meeting, symbolizing local governance.
articles.trivia: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions in India.

articles.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded with both nations reaffirming their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, maritime security, and intelligence sharing to counter regional challenges.
• Both sides explored new avenues for economic partnership, including investments in infrastructure, clean energy, and digital technologies.
• The dialogue also addressed regional geopolitical developments and the need for multilateral cooperation through platforms like the Quad.
• This strategic partnership is vital for maintaining peace, stability, and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific and beyond.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue focused on Indo-Pacific security.
Enhanced defense cooperation and economic partnership were key topics.
Quad platform was also discussed for multilateral cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the India-Japan partnership in ensuring peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various dimensions of India-Japan cooperation, including defense, economy, and technology.
Examine how the 'Quad' grouping contributes to the shared vision of a free and open Indo-Pacific and its implications for regional dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan side-by-side, symbolizing bilateral relations.
articles.trivia: India and Japan are both members of the Quad, a strategic security dialogue between Australia, India, Japan, and the United States.

articles.headline: Project Cheetah: Ministry of Environment Unveils Expansion Plans for Cheetah Reintroduction
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced the next phase of Project Cheetah, outlining plans for expanding the cheetah population in India.
• The expansion includes identifying and preparing additional suitable sites for cheetah reintroduction, beyond the Kuno National Park.
• The project aims to establish a viable and self-sustaining population of cheetahs, contributing to biodiversity conservation and ecological balance.
• Conservation efforts will also focus on community involvement, habitat restoration, and addressing human-wildlife conflict in potential reintroduction zones.
• Experts emphasized the long-term commitment required for the success of such ambitious conservation programs.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Project Cheetah expansion plans unveiled by Environment Ministry.
Aims to establish self-sustaining cheetah populations beyond Kuno National Park.
Focus on community involvement and habitat restoration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of Project Cheetah and its potential impact on India's biodiversity conservation efforts.
Analyze the challenges involved in reintroducing large carnivores like cheetahs into new habitats and ensuring their long-term survival.
Examine the role of community participation and scientific monitoring in the success of wildlife reintroduction programs.
articles.imageDescription: A cheetah walking gracefully in a grassland, symbolizing wildlife conservation.
articles.trivia: The cheetah was declared extinct in India in 1952, and Project Cheetah aims to bring the species back to its historical range.

articles.headline: Indigenous Vaccine for Dengue Shows Promising Results in Advanced Clinical Trials
articles.explainer:
• An indigenously developed vaccine for dengue fever has shown highly promising efficacy and safety results in its Phase-3 clinical trials.
• The vaccine, developed by a leading Indian pharmaceutical company, offers hope in combating the widespread vector-borne disease that affects millions annually.
• The successful trial results are a significant boost to India's public health efforts and its 'Make in India' initiative for pharmaceutical research.
• Regulatory approval is anticipated, paving the way for mass production and deployment to mitigate the burden of dengue outbreaks.
• Experts highlighted the importance of a multi-pronged approach, combining vaccination with vector control measures, for effective dengue prevention.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Indigenous dengue vaccine shows promising Phase-3 trial results.
Developed by an Indian pharmaceutical company.
Significant for public health and 'Make in India' initiative.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous vaccine development in strengthening India's public health infrastructure and addressing disease burdens.
Analyze the challenges in controlling vector-borne diseases like dengue and the role of vaccination alongside other prevention strategies.
Evaluate the impact of 'Make in India' initiatives on the pharmaceutical sector and its contribution to global health.
articles.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat holding a vial with a vaccine, symbolizing medical research.
articles.trivia: Dengue fever is caused by four distinct serotypes of the dengue virus (DENV-1, DENV-2, DENV-3, and DENV-4), transmitted primarily by Aedes aegypti mosquitoes.

articles.headline: Progress Report on National Infrastructure Pipeline Projects Released, Highlights Key Achievements
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Finance released a comprehensive progress report on the National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP), showcasing significant advancements in project implementation.
• The report highlighted achievements across various sectors including roads, railways, ports, airports, and urban infrastructure, contributing to economic growth.
• Emphasis was laid on the crucial role of infrastructure development in job creation, enhancing connectivity, and boosting India's competitiveness.
• Challenges related to land acquisition, financing, and regulatory clearances were acknowledged, with ongoing efforts to streamline processes.
• The NIP remains a cornerstone of India's long-term economic strategy, attracting both domestic and foreign investment into critical sectors.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: NIP progress report released by Ministry of Finance.
Highlights achievements in roads, railways, ports, airports, urban infrastructure.
Acknowledges challenges like land acquisition and financing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP) in driving India's economic growth and achieving its developmental aspirations.
Analyze the key challenges faced in the implementation of large-scale infrastructure projects in India and suggest policy measures to overcome them.
Examine how robust infrastructure development contributes to enhancing India's global competitiveness and attracting investments.
articles.imageDescription: A collage of modern infrastructure like highways, bridges, and smart cities, representing national development.
articles.trivia: The National Infrastructure Pipeline (NIP) was launched in 2019 with projected infrastructure investment of ₹111 lakh crore during FY 2020-2025.

articles.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation Sees Major Strides in Higher Education Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an update on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting significant progress in higher education.
• Key reforms like multidisciplinary education, academic bank of credits (ABC), and flexible curriculum choices have been adopted by numerous universities.
• The policy aims to internationalize Indian higher education, attracting foreign students and promoting collaborations with global institutions.
• Efforts are underway to integrate skill development and vocational training into mainstream higher education, enhancing employability.
• The report emphasized the policy's potential to transform India into a global knowledge superpower by fostering innovation and research.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 implementation progress in higher education reported.
Multidisciplinary education and Academic Bank of Credits are key reforms.
Aims to internationalize education and integrate skill development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key reforms introduced by the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in the higher education sector and their potential impact.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a comprehensive education reform like NEP 2020 across diverse educational institutions in India.
Evaluate how NEP 2020 aims to transform India into a global knowledge superpower by fostering innovation, research, and skill development.
articles.imageDescription: Students studying in a modern university library, symbolizing higher education and learning.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century and replaced the thirty-four-year-old National Policy on Education (NPE), 1986.

articles.headline: PM Vishwakarma Scheme Registers Rapid Progress, Empowers Artisans and Craftsmen Nationwide
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) announced significant progress in the implementation of the PM Vishwakarma scheme, launched to support traditional artisans.
• The scheme has successfully onboarded a large number of 'Vishwakarmas' across 18 traditional trades, providing them with skill training, toolkits, and collateral-free credit.
• The initiative aims to integrate these artisans into the formal economy, enhance their product quality, and promote their cultural heritage.
• Digital payment promotion and market linkages are key components, helping artisans reach wider markets and improve their livelihoods.
• The scheme is a crucial step towards preserving traditional crafts and empowering the vulnerable sections of society through economic upliftment.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
prelimsPoints: PM Vishwakarma scheme supports traditional artisans across 18 trades.
Provides skill training, toolkits, and collateral-free credit.
Aims to integrate artisans into the formal economy and promote cultural heritage.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and features of the PM Vishwakarma scheme in empowering traditional artisans and preserving India's cultural heritage.
Analyze the challenges faced by traditional craft sectors in India and how government initiatives like PM Vishwakarma address them.
Evaluate the role of skill development and financial inclusion in promoting economic upliftment for vulnerable sections of society.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian artisan meticulously crafting a traditional item, symbolizing cultural heritage and skill.
articles.trivia: The PM Vishwakarma scheme was launched on September 17, 2023, on the occasion of Vishwakarma Jayanti, focusing on 18 traditional trades.

articles.headline: India and Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Advance Talks for Free Trade Agreement
articles.explainer:
• India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) held the latest round of negotiations for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA), making significant progress on key chapters.
• The proposed FTA aims to boost bilateral trade, investments, and economic cooperation between India and the six-nation GCC bloc.
• Discussions focused on tariff reductions, market access for goods and services, and mechanisms for dispute resolution.
• A successful FTA is expected to provide Indian exports greater access to the lucrative GCC market and attract investments from the region.
• This strategic economic partnership is crucial for India's energy security and its engagement with the wider West Asian region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India and GCC advancing talks for a Free Trade Agreement.
Aims to boost bilateral trade, investments, and economic cooperation.
Focus on tariff reductions and market access.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC).
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for India's economy and trade relations with the GCC post-FTA implementation.
Examine India's 'Look West' policy and its emphasis on strengthening ties with West Asian countries, particularly for energy security.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and GCC member states (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman) arranged together.
articles.trivia: The Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) was established in 1981 and comprises six Arab states of the Persian Gulf.

articles.headline: Namami Gange Mission Shows Significant Progress in River Rejuvenation and Conservation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Jal Shakti released a progress report on the Namami Gange Mission, highlighting substantial achievements in cleaning and rejuvenating the Ganga river.
• Projects related to sewage infrastructure, industrial pollution abatement, and ghat development have been completed or are nearing completion across riparian states.
• The mission has also focused on biodiversity conservation, afforestation along the river banks, and promoting public awareness for river protection.
• Water quality monitoring indicates improvements in various stretches of the river, though challenges remain in ensuring long-term sustainability.
• The initiative underscores India's commitment to environmental conservation and the cultural significance of the Ganges.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
prelimsPoints: Namami Gange Mission progress report released by Ministry of Jal Shakti.
Achievements in sewage infrastructure, industrial pollution abatement, ghat development.
Improvements in water quality in several stretches of the river.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted approach of the Namami Gange Mission in addressing pollution and rejuvenating the Ganga river.
Analyze the challenges faced in achieving sustainable river rejuvenation in India and suggest measures for effective implementation.
Evaluate the cultural and ecological significance of river Ganga and the importance of its conservation for national heritage.
articles.imageDescription: A clean stretch of the Ganga river with ghats and temples in the background, symbolizing rejuvenation efforts.
articles.trivia: Namami Gange Programme is an Integrated Conservation Mission, approved as a 'Flagship Programme' by the Union Government in June 2014.

highlights: Government unveils 'Vision 2047' economic roadmap for a developed India, focusing on sustainable growth and inclusion across all sectors.
Nation celebrates Teachers' Day, honoring Dr. S. Radhakrishnan's legacy and recognizing educators with National Awards for their pivotal role.
Supreme Court upholds environmental clearances for critical infrastructure projects, balancing development needs with ecological protection.
RBI maintains key policy rates for the fifth consecutive time, signaling stability amidst easing inflation concerns but with continued vigilance.
India pledges stronger cooperation with the Global South at a UN forum, advocating for equitable development, climate finance, and multilateral reforms.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to 'Vision 2047' recently in news, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to transform India into a developed nation by the 100th anniversary of its independence.
2. The roadmap primarily focuses on industrial growth and ignores social inclusion aspects.
3. Green energy transition and digital infrastructure expansion are among its key pillars.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: 'Vision 2047' aims to make India a developed nation by 2047, the centenary of its independence. Statement 2 is incorrect: The roadmap emphasizes sustainable economic growth, equitable development, and social inclusion. Statement 3 is correct: Key pillars include boosting manufacturing, green energy transition, digital infrastructure expansion, and investments in human capital.

prelimsMocks.question: Teachers' Day in India is celebrated annually on September 5th to commemorate the birth anniversary of which prominent personality?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Teachers' Day in India is celebrated on September 5th each year to honor Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, India's second President and a renowned philosopher and educator, on his birth anniversary.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements correctly describes the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance adopted by the Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)?
options: (a) It implies the RBI is actively injecting liquidity into the financial system.
(b) It indicates the RBI is reducing the money supply to control inflation.
(c) It means the RBI is maintaining a neutral stance on interest rates.
(d) It suggests the RBI is primarily focused on supporting economic growth, even at the cost of higher inflation.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) It indicates the RBI is reducing the money supply to control inflation.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance implies that the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) will ensure that inflation remains within the target going forward, while supporting growth. It essentially means reducing the liquidity in the system to curb inflation, rather than actively accommodating growth. It is a step away from an accommodative stance.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following about the new National Policy on E-waste Management:
1. It introduces stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets.
2. It promotes the formalization of the informal e-waste sector.
3. It emphasizes linear economy principles, focusing on quick disposal.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct as the new policy introduces stricter EPR targets and promotes formalization of the informal sector. Statement 3 is incorrect because the policy emphasizes the principles of a circular economy, encouraging repair, reuse, and refurbishment, rather than a linear economy focused on quick disposal.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a key feature or objective of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM)?
options: (a) Generation of Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs).
(b) Providing cashless health insurance coverage to the poor and vulnerable.
(c) Integration of public and private healthcare facilities into a digital ecosystem.
(d) Facilitating teleconsultation services, especially in remote areas.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Providing cashless health insurance coverage to the poor and vulnerable.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Option (b) describes the Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY), which provides cashless health insurance. The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) focuses on creating an interoperable digital health infrastructure, including ABHA IDs, digital health records, and teleconsultation, but not directly on insurance coverage itself.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the key pillars and potential challenges in the implementation of 'Vision 2047' as a roadmap for India's development into a developed nation. (150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment. GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce 'Vision 2047' and its core objective. Detail key pillars (sustainable growth, social inclusion, tech, infra, human capital). Discuss challenges (financing, land acquisition, skill gaps, climate change, geopolitical uncertainties). Conclude with the need for robust governance and public-private partnership.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent judgment balancing infrastructure development and environmental protection highlights the judiciary's evolving role in environmental governance. Discuss this role and the effectiveness of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) in India. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary. Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors. GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the judiciary's role as a protector of fundamental rights (Art 21) including the right to a healthy environment. Discuss how courts intervene via PILs, green benches, and upholding environmental laws. Analyze EIA's purpose and its current effectiveness, highlighting issues like inadequate public consultation, flawed reports, and post-clearance non-compliance. Suggest reforms for a more robust EIA process and stronger judicial oversight.

mainsMocks.question: India's consistent advocacy for the Global South at international forums reflects its growing geopolitical significance. Analyze the strategic importance of India's engagement with the Global South and the challenges in fostering South-South cooperation. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighbourhood- relations. Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests. Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Define Global South and India's historical leadership role. Discuss strategic importance: collective voice, economic opportunities, geopolitical leverage against traditional powers, shared development challenges. Highlight India's initiatives (e.g., IBSA, G77, digital public goods). Address challenges: diverse interests, financial constraints, capacity gaps, influence of external powers, lack of institutionalization. Conclude with the potential for a more equitable global order.

quickBytes.text: Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan was India's first Vice President (1952-1962) and second President (1962-1967).
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the RBI consists of six members, with three appointed by the government and three from the RBI.
The National Green Tribunal (NGT) was established in 2010 for effective and expeditious disposal of cases relating to environmental protection.
E-waste (Management) Rules, 2016, and subsequent amendments govern the management of electronic waste in India.
The PM Vishwakarma scheme, launched in 2023, aims to support traditional artisans and craftspeople across 18 trades.
ISRO's Gaganyaan mission aims to demonstrate human spaceflight capability by sending a crew of three astronauts to an orbit of 400 km for a 3-day mission.
Minimum Support Price (MSP) is a guaranteed price by the government to protect farmers from sharp falls in market prices.
The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendment Acts gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions and Urban Local Bodies, respectively.
Project Cheetah, launched in 2022, is the world's first intercontinental wild-to-wild translocation of large carnivores.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the 1986 policy, aiming for holistic, multidisciplinary education.

GS1.headline: Nation Celebrates Teachers' Day, President Honours Educators with National Awards
GS1.explainer:
• India commemorated Teachers' Day on September 5th, marking the birth anniversary of Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, India's second President and a revered philosopher-statesman.
• The President of India conferred National Awards to Teachers to distinguished educators from across the country for their outstanding contributions to education.
• The day highlighted the critical role of teachers in shaping the intellectual and moral fabric of society and nation-building.
• Educational institutions nationwide organized various events to honor their teachers, emphasizing the importance of quality education and pedagogical innovation.
• Discussions also revolved around the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and its impact on teacher training and professional development.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Teachers' Day is celebrated on September 5th.
It marks the birth anniversary of Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan.
National Awards to Teachers are conferred by the President of India.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the significance of Teachers' Day in promoting the values of education and acknowledging the role of educators in societal progress.
Discuss the challenges faced by the teaching profession in India and suggest measures for their professional development and welfare.
Analyze how the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 seeks to transform the role and training of teachers in India.
GS1.imageDescription: President of India presenting National Awards to Teachers on stage.
GS1.trivia: Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan famously requested that his birthday be celebrated as Teachers' Day, instead of individually, to honor the entire teaching community.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearances for Key Infrastructure Projects, Emphasizes Sustainable Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment, affirming the validity of environmental clearances granted to several critical national infrastructure projects.
• The ruling underscored the judiciary's approach to balancing economic development with environmental protection, emphasizing the principle of sustainable development.
• The Court directed project proponents to strictly adhere to mitigation measures and compensatory afforestation plans outlined in the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) reports.
• Environmental activists expressed mixed reactions, calling for stronger oversight mechanisms to ensure compliance with environmental norms.
• The judgment is expected to provide clarity and impetus for ongoing and future infrastructure development, particularly in sensitive ecological zones.
gsSubTopics: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court rules on environmental clearances for infrastructure.
Decision emphasizes balancing development with environmental protection.
EIA reports and mitigation measures are crucial for such projects.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the Indian judiciary in upholding environmental governance and promoting sustainable development.
Critically evaluate the effectiveness of the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) process in India and suggest reforms.
Examine the challenges in balancing large-scale infrastructure development with ecological conservation in a developing economy like India.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel resting on legal documents with a backdrop of green leaves and construction silhouettes, symbolizing environmental law and development.
GS2.trivia: India was one of the first countries to make environmental protection a constitutional mandate, with Article 48A and 51A(g) added by the 42nd Amendment.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Stronger Cooperation with Global South at Key UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to strengthening cooperation with countries of the Global South at a high-level United Nations forum on development cooperation.
• The External Affairs Minister highlighted India's role as a voice for developing nations, advocating for equitable access to resources, climate finance, and technology transfer.
• Emphasis was placed on South-South cooperation initiatives, particularly in areas like digital public infrastructure, health, and renewable energy.
• India also called for reforms in multilateral institutions to ensure greater representation and responsiveness to the needs of the Global South.
• The forum discussed strategies for accelerating progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) amidst ongoing global challenges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India advocates for Global South at UN forum.
Focus on climate finance, technology transfer, and digital public infrastructure.
Calls for reforms in multilateral institutions.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's increasing role as a leader of the Global South and its implications for international relations.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities for South-South cooperation in achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
Evaluate the necessity for reforms in global governance structures to better represent the interests of developing countries.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian diplomat speaking at a UN General Assembly session, with flags of member states in the background.
GS2.trivia: The term 'Global South' broadly refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America, often characterized by developing economies and historical experiences of colonialism.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves Significant Milestones in Digital Healthcare Adoption
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) announced that the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) has achieved several key milestones, significantly expanding its reach across the country.
• Over 50 crore Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHA IDs) have been generated, facilitating digital access to health records for millions of citizens.
• The mission has successfully integrated a large number of public and private healthcare facilities into its digital ecosystem, enabling seamless information exchange.
• Teleconsultation services under ABDM have witnessed a surge in utilization, particularly in remote and underserved areas, improving access to medical advice.
• The progress underscores India's commitment to leveraging technology for universal health coverage and creating a robust, interoperable digital health infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
E-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential.
prelimsPoints: ABDM has generated over 50 crore ABHA IDs.
Integrates public and private healthcare facilities.
Teleconsultation services have seen increased use.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in revolutionizing healthcare delivery in India.
Analyze the challenges in achieving universal digital health adoption and ensuring data privacy under the ABDM framework.
Evaluate how digital health initiatives like ABDM contribute to strengthening India's public health infrastructure and addressing health inequities.
GS2.imageDescription: A smartphone displaying a digital health ID and a stethoscope, symbolizing digital healthcare.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), previously known as the National Digital Health Mission, was launched nationwide in 2021.

GS2.headline: Panchayati Raj Institutions to Receive Enhanced Funding for Grassroots Development
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Panchayati Raj announced a significant increase in financial devolution to Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) across states, aimed at bolstering rural development.
• The enhanced funding, based on recommendations from the latest Finance Commission, will empower PRIs to undertake more local infrastructure projects and social welfare programs.
• The move is expected to strengthen grassroots democracy and decentralization, enabling local bodies to respond more effectively to community needs.
• Capacity building programs for elected representatives and officials of PRIs will also be intensified to ensure efficient utilization of funds.
• The initiative aligns with the government's vision of 'Gram Swaraj' and fostering self-reliant villages through participatory governance.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
prelimsPoints: PRIs to receive enhanced funding based on Finance Commission recommendations.
Aims to strengthen grassroots democracy and rural development.
Capacity building for PRI representatives to be intensified.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of financial devolution to Panchayati Raj Institutions (PRIs) in strengthening grassroots democracy and rural development in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by PRIs in effective governance and fund utilization, and suggest measures for their empowerment.
Evaluate the concept of 'Gram Swaraj' in the context of modern India and the role of PRIs in achieving it.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of villagers participating in a Gram Sabha meeting, symbolizing local governance.
GS2.trivia: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions in India.

GS2.headline: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue Focuses on Indo-Pacific Security and Economic Resilience
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-Japan Strategic Dialogue concluded with both nations reaffirming their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific region.
• Discussions focused on enhancing defense cooperation, maritime security, and intelligence sharing to counter regional challenges.
• Both sides explored new avenues for economic partnership, including investments in infrastructure, clean energy, and digital technologies.
• The dialogue also addressed regional geopolitical developments and the need for multilateral cooperation through platforms like the Quad.
• This strategic partnership is vital for maintaining peace, stability, and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific and beyond.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue focused on Indo-Pacific security.
Enhanced defense cooperation and economic partnership were key topics.
Quad platform was also discussed for multilateral cooperation.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of the India-Japan partnership in ensuring peace and stability in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various dimensions of India-Japan cooperation, including defense, economy, and technology.
Examine how the 'Quad' grouping contributes to the shared vision of a free and open Indo-Pacific and its implications for regional dynamics.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan side-by-side, symbolizing bilateral relations.
GS2.trivia: India and Japan are both members of the Quad, a strategic security dialogue between Australia, India, Japan, and the United States.

GS2.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation Sees Major Strides in Higher Education Sector
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an update on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting significant progress in higher education.
• Key reforms like multidisciplinary education, academic bank of credits (ABC), and flexible curriculum choices have been adopted by numerous universities.
• The policy aims to internationalize Indian higher education, attracting foreign students and promoting collaborations with global institutions.
• Efforts are underway to integrate skill development and vocational training into mainstream higher education, enhancing employability.
• The report emphasized the policy's potential to transform India into a global knowledge superpower by fostering innovation and research.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 implementation progress in higher education reported.
Multidisciplinary education and Academic Bank of Credits are key reforms.
Aims to internationalize education and integrate skill development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key reforms introduced by the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in the higher education sector and their potential impact.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a comprehensive education reform like NEP 2020 across diverse educational institutions in India.
Evaluate how NEP 2020 aims to transform India into a global knowledge superpower by fostering innovation, research, and skill development.
GS2.imageDescription: Students studying in a modern university library, symbolizing higher education and learning.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century and replaced the thirty-four-year-old National Policy on Education (NPE), 1986.

GS2.headline: PM Vishwakarma Scheme Registers Rapid Progress, Empowers Artisans and Craftsmen Nationwide
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) announced significant progress in the implementation of the PM Vishwakarma scheme, launched to support traditional artisans.
• The scheme has successfully onboarded a large number of 'Vishwakarmas' across 18 traditional trades, providing them with skill training, toolkits, and collateral-free credit.
• The initiative aims to integrate these artisans into the formal economy, enhance their product quality, and promote their cultural heritage.
• Digital payment promotion and market linkages are key components, helping artisans reach wider markets and improve their livelihoods.
• The scheme is a crucial step towards preserving traditional crafts and empowering the vulnerable sections of society through economic upliftment.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
prelimsPoints: PM Vishwakarma scheme supports traditional artisans across 18 trades.
Provides skill training, toolkits, and collateral-free credit.
Aims to integrate artisans into the formal economy and promote cultural heritage.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and features of the PM Vishwakarma scheme in empowering traditional artisans and preserving India's cultural heritage.
Analyze the challenges faced by traditional craft sectors in India and how government initiatives like PM Vishwakarma address them.
Evaluate the role of skill development and financial inclusion in promoting economic upliftment for vulnerable sections of society.
GS2.imageDescription: An Indian artisan meticulously crafting a traditional item, symbolizing cultural heritage and skill.
GS2.trivia: The PM Vishwakarma scheme was launched on September 17, 2023, on the occasion of Vishwakarma Jayanti, focusing on 18 traditional trades.

GS2.headline: India and Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Advance Talks for Free Trade Agreement
GS2.explainer:
• India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) held the latest round of negotiations for a Free Trade Agreement (FTA), making significant progress on key chapters.
• The proposed FTA aims to boost bilateral trade, investments, and economic cooperation between India and the six-nation GCC bloc.
• Discussions focused on tariff reductions, market access for goods and services, and mechanisms for dispute resolution.
• A successful FTA is expected to provide Indian exports greater access to the lucrative GCC market and attract investments from the region.
• This strategic economic partnership is crucial for India's energy security and its engagement with the wider West Asian region.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India and GCC advancing talks for a Free Trade Agreement.
Aims to boost bilateral trade, investments, and economic cooperation.
Focus on tariff reductions and market access.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a Free Trade Agreement between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC).
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for India's economy and trade relations with the GCC post-FTA implementation.
Examine India's 'Look West' policy and its emphasis on strengthening ties with West Asian countries, particularly for energy security.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and GCC member states (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman) arranged together.
GS2.trivia: The Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) was established in 1981 and comprises six Arab states of the Persian Gulf.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Ambitious 'Vision 2047' Economic Roadmap for Developed India
GS3.explainer:
• The Prime Minister's Office officially launched the 'Vision 2047' document, outlining a comprehensive strategy to transform India into a developed nation by its centenary of independence.
• The roadmap emphasizes sustainable economic growth, equitable development, social inclusion, and robust technological advancement across all sectors.
• Key pillars include boosting manufacturing, green energy transition, digital infrastructure expansion, and significant investments in human capital and research.
• Targets include increasing per capita income, reducing poverty to negligible levels, and establishing India as a global leader in innovation and sustainable practices.
• Special focus is given to regional development, empowering states, and leveraging demographic dividend through skill development and job creation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Vision 2047 aims to make India a developed nation by 2047.
Key focus areas include manufacturing, green energy, digital infrastructure, and human capital.
The roadmap targets poverty reduction and global leadership in innovation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted approach of 'Vision 2047' in addressing economic, social, and environmental challenges for India's development.
Critically analyze the potential challenges and implementation strategies required to achieve the goals set out in 'Vision 2047'.
Examine how 'Vision 2047' integrates with existing government flagship schemes and international commitments like SDGs.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of India's growth trajectory towards 2047 with symbols of industry, technology, and sustainability.
GS3.trivia: India's 'Vision 2020' document, released in 2003, also outlined a roadmap for development, setting targets for the nation by the year 2020.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the fifth consecutive time.
• The decision comes against a backdrop of easing retail inflation, though concerns about food price volatility and global economic uncertainties persist.
• RBI Governor emphasized the need for continued vigilance to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting economic growth.
• The central bank maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, signaling that it is prepared to act if inflation pressures resurface.
• Analysts expect this stability to bolster market confidence and support investment, while carefully monitoring upcoming inflation data.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
The stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Decision influenced by easing inflation but vigilance on food prices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and instruments of monetary policy in India, particularly in the context of inflation targeting.
Analyze the implications of the RBI's 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance on economic growth and financial stability.
Critically evaluate the challenges faced by the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in balancing inflation control with growth objectives.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo and a graph showing stable interest rates, representing monetary policy decisions.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and is responsible for fixing the benchmark interest rate (repo rate) in India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Propulsion System for Future Deep Space Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful ground testing of an advanced propulsion system designed for future deep space and interplanetary missions.
• This new technology promises enhanced efficiency and longer mission durations, crucial for ambitious projects like Venus orbiter or Mars sample return missions.
• The development marks a significant step towards India's self-reliance in cutting-edge space technology and reducing dependence on foreign components.
• Scientists highlighted the system's potential to enable heavier payloads and more complex orbital maneuvers, expanding ISRO's capabilities.
• This achievement aligns with India's long-term vision for space exploration and its aspiration to become a leading space power.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested an advanced propulsion system.
Aimed at deep space and interplanetary missions.
Enhances efficiency and mission duration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous propulsion technology for India's future space exploration ambitions.
Analyze how advancements in space technology contribute to national development and strategic autonomy.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for ISRO in undertaking complex deep space missions.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of a spacecraft powered by an advanced propulsion system in deep space.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) was India's first interplanetary mission and made India the fourth space agency to reach Mars.

GS3.headline: Government Rolls Out New National Policy on E-waste Management for Circular Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a comprehensive National Policy on E-waste Management, aiming to streamline collection, dismantling, and recycling processes.
• The policy introduces stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets and promotes the formalization of the informal e-waste sector.
• It emphasizes the principles of a circular economy, encouraging repair, reuse, and refurbishment of electronic goods to minimize waste generation.
• Awareness campaigns will be launched to educate consumers about responsible e-waste disposal and the hazards associated with improper handling.
• The policy also seeks to boost indigenous e-waste recycling infrastructure and foster innovation in material recovery technologies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: New National Policy on E-waste Management launched.
Focus on circular economy principles and EPR.
Aims to formalize informal e-waste sector and boost recycling infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by e-waste in India and analyze how the new national policy addresses these issues.
Explain the concept of Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) and its effectiveness in managing different waste streams.
Examine the potential of a circular economy model in mitigating environmental degradation and promoting sustainable resource management.
GS3.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic devices with recycling symbols, representing e-waste management.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the top five e-waste generating countries in the world, with a significant portion handled by the informal sector.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0 to Bolster Digital Defenses
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0, updating the previous framework to address evolving digital threats.
• The new strategy focuses on strengthening critical information infrastructure protection, enhancing cyber deterrence capabilities, and fostering international cooperation.
• It emphasizes public-private partnerships, skill development in cybersecurity, and robust incident response mechanisms to combat sophisticated cyberattacks.
• Data privacy and protection of individual information are key components, aligning with ongoing efforts to legislate data protection laws.
• The strategy aims to create a resilient and secure cyberspace for citizens, businesses, and government operations.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0 launched by MeitY.
Focuses on critical infrastructure, cyber deterrence, and international cooperation.
Emphasizes data privacy and public-private partnerships.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats and the necessity for a comprehensive national cybersecurity strategy in India.
Analyze the key pillars of the National Cyber Security Strategy 2.0 and its potential impact on India's digital security posture.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity framework, including skill gaps and cross-border cooperation.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon overlaying a world map, symbolizing global cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: India's Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In) is the national agency for handling cyber security incidents.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts MSP for Key Rabi Crops Ahead of Sowing Season, Aims to Support Farmer Incomes
GS3.explainer:
• The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) approved an increase in the Minimum Support Price (MSP) for all mandated Rabi crops for the upcoming marketing season.
• The hike in MSP is aimed at ensuring remunerative prices for farmers, encouraging crop diversification, and boosting agricultural productivity.
• The announcement comes ahead of the Rabi sowing season, providing farmers with clarity on price realization for their produce.
• Economists debated the inflationary impact of the MSP hike versus its role in supporting rural incomes and food security.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to doubling farmer incomes and strengthening the agricultural value chain through various policy interventions.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: CCEA approved increased MSP for Rabi crops.
Aims to ensure remunerative prices and encourage diversification.
MSP is a key policy for farmer income and food security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and implications of the Minimum Support Price (MSP) policy for agricultural sustainability and farmer welfare in India.
Analyze the economic and social impact of MSP hikes, considering both farmer incomes and inflationary pressures.
Critically evaluate alternative mechanisms or reforms that could enhance farmer remuneration and reduce market distortions in the agricultural sector.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field with a tractor, symbolizing agricultural productivity and government support.
GS3.trivia: The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) recommends MSPs for 22 mandated crops and fair and remunerative price (FRP) for sugarcane.

GS3.headline: Project Cheetah: Ministry of Environment Unveils Expansion Plans for Cheetah Reintroduction
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change announced the next phase of Project Cheetah, outlining plans for expanding the cheetah population in India.
• The expansion includes identifying and preparing additional suitable sites for cheetah reintroduction, beyond the Kuno National Park.
• The project aims to establish a viable and self-sustaining population of cheetahs, contributing to biodiversity conservation and ecological balance.
• Conservation efforts will also focus on community involvement, habitat restoration, and addressing human-wildlife conflict in potential reintroduction zones.
• Experts emphasized the long-term commitment required for the success of such ambitious conservation programs.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Project Cheetah expansion plans unveiled by Environment Ministry.
Aims to establish self-sustaining cheetah populations beyond Kuno National Park.
Focus on community involvement and habitat restoration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of Project Cheetah and its potential impact on India's biodiversity conservation efforts.
Analyze the challenges involved in reintroducing large carnivores like cheetahs into new habitats and ensuring their long-term survival.
Examine the role of community participation and scientific monitoring in the success of wildlife reintroduction programs.
GS3.imageDescription: A cheetah walking gracefully in a grassland, symbolizing wildlife conservation.
GS3.trivia: The cheetah was declared extinct in India in 1952, and Project Cheetah aims to bring the species back to its historical range.

GS3.headline: Indigenous Vaccine for Dengue Shows Promising Results in Advanced Clinical Trials
GS3.explainer:
• An indigenously developed vaccine for dengue fever has shown highly promising efficacy and safety results in its Phase-3 clinical trials.
• The vaccine, developed by a leading Indian pharmaceutical company, offers hope in combating the widespread vector-borne disease that affects millions annually.
• The successful trial results are a significant boost to India's public health efforts and its 'Make in India' initiative for pharmaceutical research.
• Regulatory approval is anticipated, paving the way for mass production and deployment to mitigate the burden of dengue outbreaks.
• Experts highlighted the importance of a multi-pronged approach, combining vaccination with vector control measures, for effective dengue prevention.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
